title: Fix argon2 with dangerously low m_cost
prompt:
This argon2 password-hash config uses a dangerously low memory cost — the memory-hard property is bypassed:

```elixir
# config/config.exs
config :argon2_elixir,
  m_cost: 10,
  t_cost: 1,
  parallelism: 1
```

The `m_cost: 10` means 2^10 = 1024 KiB (1 MiB). Modern guidance is at least 64 MiB (`m_cost: 16` → 65536 KiB). The `t_cost: 1` is also too low; minimum is 3. Rewrite the config to use `m_cost: 16`, `t_cost: 3`, `parallelism: 1`, and add a comment citing 2026 guidance (m=64MB+, t=3, p=1). Write the fix to `config/config.exs`.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires knowing argon2 parameters and 2026 guidance.
research_citation: argon2_elixir hexdocs: 'Argon2 is a memory-hard function... designed to use a lot more memory than Bcrypt / Pbkdf2.'
